must_find.description: lock_sync() blocks the tokio runtime thread inside an async task; should use async-aware locking or spawn_blocking
Mutex guard is held for the entire batch flush loop (up to 50 DB writes), starving other tasks that need the database
Events only flush on batch capacity (50); no time-based flush means events can be buffered indefinitely during low throughput

must_not_flag.description: emit_event uses an unbounded channel send which is non-blocking and appropriate for this fire-and-forget pattern
broadcast_ws uses tokio broadcast::Sender which is lock-free and appropriate for fan-out messaging
